finding: A native git commit under ATM pre-commit scoped-index governance can report ATM_HOOK_PRE_COMMIT_OK with stagedFileCount > 0, yet produce an empty commit while the intended files remain dirty in the live worktree.
expectedBehavior: When ATM uses a scoped index or temp-index path, pre-commit should fail closed if the host git commit will not include the reported staged files, or should surface an explicit recovery command before allowing an empty commit.
evidenceOrRepro: During the ATM-BUG-2026-07-16-015 fix, native `git commit -m "fix(runner-sync): admit owned steward queue file"` reported ATM_HOOK_PRE_COMMIT_OK with four staged files, but `git show --name-status HEAD` showed no changed files and `git diff HEAD --name-only` still listed the four intended files. A follow-up `git commit --amend --no-edit` with ATM_COMMIT_ACTOR_ID set included the files.
followUp: Add a regression for native git commit plus ATM scoped-index pre-commit behavior, then ensure empty commits are rejected when staged deliverables were reported or make the hook emit a precise amend/retry command.
